query: How frequently does the CDC issue new Travel Health Notices for North American countries (Mexico, Canada, Caribbean, Central America)? List recent CDC travel health notices issued in 2025 and 2026 for North American countries with their issue dates. What is the typical monthly rate of new notices affecting North America?
answer:
I've completed the research on CDC Travel Health Notices for North American countries. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**How CDC issues THNs:** CDC posts Travel Health Notices on a 4-level scale (Level 1 = Usual Precautions through Level 4 = Avoid All Travel), triggered by outbreaks, sporadic cases in new locations, events, or natural disasters. These are separate from State Department Travel Advisories and CDC HAN advisories.

**Recent North America-affecting notices (2025–2026):**
- **June 25, 2026** — Diphtheria, Haiti (Level 1, New)
- **June 4, 2026** — Hepatitis A, Canada/Manitoba (Level 1, New)
- **Dec 23, 2025** — Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever, Mexico northern border states (Level 1)
- **Sept 18, 2025** — Oropouche in the Americas, incl. Cuba & Panama (Level 1; Cuba at Level 2)
- **May 28, 2025** — Global Measles, listing nearly all N. American/Caribbean/Central American countries (Level 1)
- **May 30, 2024** — Salmonella Newport, Mexico (Level 1, still active)

**Monthly rate:** Roughly **0.3–0.5 new North America-affecting notices per month** — about one new region-specific notice every 2–3 months. Globally, CDC posts several notices monthly (~8 in June 2026 alone), but region-specific ones cluster around outbreak events rather than a steady cadence.

**Key caveats:** Counting methodology heavily affects the "rate" since umbrella notices (Global Measles, Global Dengue, Oropouche in the Americas) list many North American countries as a single notice. Also, New World Screwworm in Central America/Mexico has been handled mainly through HAN advisories and destination-page alerts rather than a standalone leveled THN.

Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.snippet: Full list of current CDC Travel Health Notices with levels and dates, including Diphtheria in Haiti (June 25, 2026), Hepatitis A in Canada/Manitoba (June 4, 2026), RMSF in Mexico (Dec 23, 2025), Oropouche in the Americas (Sept 18, 2025), Global Measles (May 28, 2025), Salmonella Newport in Mexico (May 30, 2024).

sources.title: Oropouche in Cuba - Level 2 - Travel Health Notices | CDC
sources.snippet: Oropouche in Cuba elevated to Level 2 - Practice Enhanced Precautions; remaining Americas at Level 1.

sources.title: Oropouche in the Americas - Level 1 - Travel Health Notices | CDC
sources.snippet: Areas with Oropouche cases: Brazil, Cuba, Panama, Peru.

sources.title: Mexico - Traveler view | Travelers' Health | CDC
sources.snippet: New World screwworm myiasis reported in Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama; travelers advised to avoid bug bites and cover wounds.

sources.title: CDC warns about New World screwworm just across the border in Mexico | CIDRAP
sources.snippet: CDC issued a Health Alert Network (HAN) advisory on animal cases of New World screwworm in Tamaulipas, Mexico; no US human/animal infestations as of Jan 20, 2026.

sources.title: New World Screwworm Outbreak | CDC
sources.snippet: On June 3, 2026, USDA confirmed the first US animal case in the current outbreak; no locally acquired human infestations reported.

sources.title: CDC issues Oropouche virus alerts for clinicians and travelers | CIDRAP
sources.snippet: CDC posted a HAN notice for Oropouche virus after expanded activity in Cuba and South America, with imported US cases.

sources.title: HHS and USDA Confirm Singular Traveler-Associated New World Screwworm Case | HHS.gov
sources.snippet: HHS/CDC/USDA confirmed a traveler-associated New World screwworm human case linked to the Central America outbreak (2025).
